cross_scene_arcs.title: The Reluctant Guardian: Protecting Sidney Across Time
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey's central mission evolves from shielding Sidney from media exposure to actively defending her life, culminating in his repeated physical sacrifices to ensure her survival despite personal cost.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey seeks to protect Sidney by controlling how she learns about new murders and keeping her location secret.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey survives multiple near-fatal attacks to directly intervene in Sidney's final confrontation, reaffirming his role as her ultimate protector.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Partnership: The Push and Pull with Gale
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey and Gale's strained relationship cycles through emotional distance, professional conflict, and failed collaboration before culminating in a hard-won reconciliation after shared trauma.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey is emotionally isolated and resentful toward Gale, avoiding collaboration despite shared stakes in the investigation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After surviving life-threatening danger, Dewey proposes rekindling their romance and they reconcile emotionally.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Their long-term compatibility and ability to sustain the relationship beyond crisis remain uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Consultant to Combatant: Reclaiming the Investigator Role
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey transitions from a symbolic consultant on a Hollywood set to an active, frontline investigator confronting real danger, reclaiming his identity as a law enforcement figure in the face of escalating violence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey serves as a consultant on the Stab 3 set, symbolically detached from active duty but emotionally tied to the past.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey engages the killer directly, uses his weapon, survives an explosion, and becomes a central figure in the physical confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The commodification of his past trauma continues, blurring the line between authentic justice and performative reenactment.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Pattern Unfolds: Decoding the Killer's Script
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey confronts a killer who weaponizes narrative structure, forcing him to shift from reactive policing to predictive investigation as he uncovers the connection between the Stab script and real murders.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The resurgence of Ghostface killings linked to the Stab franchise catches Dewey off guard, reigniting his involvement.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey deciphers the killer's use of the script and Hollywood history, leading a covert operation to expose the truth—only to be incapacitated at the moment of revelation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full significance of the paternity test and the killer's ultimate motive remain obscured even as Dewey closes in on the truth.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Survival and Redemption: The Cost of Protection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey endures repeated physical and psychological trauma, appearing to be defeated before making a final return that saves Sidney and redeems his role as protector, at great personal cost.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey is captured, beaten, and used as bait, seemingly reduced to a helpless pawn in the killer's plan.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite severe injuries, Dewey returns to kill the antagonist and is reunited with Sidney and Gale, achieving emotional closure and survival against the odds.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Dewey's long-term physical recovery and ability to move beyond the cycle of violence remain uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Observer to Target: The Personalization of Danger
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale transitions from a media-savvy commentator exploiting the Woodsboro legacy to a direct target of the killer's orchestrated plot, culminating in her capture and fight for survival. Her status evolves from detached journalist to vulnerable participant in the very horror narrative she once commodified.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale leverages her fame and proximity to the Stab 3 production to maintain media relevance and control over the murder narrative.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She becomes a hunted victim, witnessing multiple murders and ultimately being captured as bait in the killer's trap.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moment when Gale shifts from being a narrative observer to a plotted victim remains psychologically unresolved—she never fully processes how her role in the media machine made her a target.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Alliance with Dewey and the Quest for Sidney
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale's strained relationship with Dewey evolves from professional exclusion and personal resentment to reluctant cooperation and, ultimately, emotional reconciliation. Their bond is tested by secrecy, danger, and shared trauma, culminating in a moment of intimate connection after survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey rebuffs Gale's attempt to help find Sidney, citing broken trust and her past sensationalism.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After surviving the final confrontation, Gale chooses emotional closeness with Dewey, symbolizing a shift from rivalry to mutual care.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The depth of Dewey's lingering resentment and whether their relationship can sustain beyond the crisis remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Uncovering the Hollywood Conspiracy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale doggedly pursues the truth behind the new murders, tracing a hidden legacy from Maureen Prescott's past to a corrupt Hollywood system. Her investigative drive leads her to uncover suppressed film history and studio complicity, positioning her as the moral center of the truth-seeking effort.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale learns the Hollywood murders are tied to the Stab films and that Sidney is in danger, prompting her to shift from reporter to investigator.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She uncovers Maureen Prescott's identity as Rina Reynolds and exposes John Milton's role in a predatory studio culture, advancing the core mystery.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: While Gale exposes the cover-up, the full extent of studio involvement and whether others conspired to silence the truth remains unproven.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Scripted Fate
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale faces a meta-level conflict: her fate is predetermined in the STAB 3 script, turning her into a character in the very horror franchise she helped create. She resists this narrative control, asserting agency through defiance, investigation, and survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale realizes she is scripted to die, reducing her to a fictional trope rather than a real person.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She defies the script by surviving the house explosion and continuing her investigation, reclaiming autonomy from the killer's narrative design.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The origin of the script and whether the killer is merely following or rewriting it remains unclear, leaving Gale's agency in tension with the story's control.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Evolution of Investigative Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale's journey from excluded journalist to indispensable investigator reflects a broader struggle for legitimacy. She overcomes institutional resistance, personal doubt, and physical danger to assert her expertise and moral stake in solving the case.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale is shut out by Dewey and lacks official standing, her knowledge dismissed as sensationalism.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She becomes a central truth-seeker, correcting law enforcement, identifying key clues, and driving the investigation forward alongside Sidney and Dewey.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite her contributions, Gale's role remains unofficial—her authority is earned but not formally recognized, leaving her position perpetually precarious.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Survivor to Investigator: Confronting the Trilogy's Truth
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney evolves from a traumatized survivor in hiding to an active agent seeking truth, driven by Randy's posthumous revelation that the killings form a trilogy rooted in a buried lie. She gradually internalizes the narrative structure of horror films, applies it to her own life, uncovers the truth about her half-brother Roman and her mother's past, and ultimately achieves closure by rejecting victimhood and reclaiming her story.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney re-enters the investigation after years of isolation, motivated by a direct threat.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sidney leaves the trauma behind with emotional peace, having confronted the truth and survived.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Haunting Legacy of Maureen: Trauma, Memory, and Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney's psychological connection to her mother, Maureen, is repeatedly invoked through nightmares, voice manipulation, and the final revelation of family secrets. The killer exploits this bond to destabilize her, but the arc culminates in Sidney gaining clarity about Maureen's abandonment of Roman, allowing her to reconcile the myth of her mother with reality and break free from the emotional chains of the past.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney is haunted by a dream of her mother being taken, reigniting unresolved grief.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sidney integrates the truth about her mother's past and finds peace, no longer defined by loss.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Escalating Threat: From Isolation to Final Confrontation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The external conflict intensifies as the killer systematically dismantles Sidney's attempts at safety, forcing her out of hiding, manipulating her through phone calls, using her loved ones as bait, and luring her into a staged final confrontation. Each escalation strips away her sense of control until she must enter the killer's domain to survive.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney is threatened by an anonymous caller, shattering her fragile safety.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sidney enters the soundstage, fully aware she is walking into a trap, but determined to face it.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The killer's identity and full plan remain unknown, maintaining suspense and danger.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reclaiming Agency: Acts of Resistance and Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney progressively reclaims personal agency through small but significant acts—insisting on going to the bathroom alone, escaping ambushes, rescuing Gale, and choosing to enter the killer's domain on her own terms. Though repeatedly cornered, her survival instincts and growing resolve transform her from a target into an active fighter.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney asserts minor independence by moving unescorted in a controlled environment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sidney infiltrates the studio lot alone, transitioning from prey to pursuer in the final act.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite her growing strength, she remains vulnerable and weaponless at key moments, underscoring the fragility of her control.
